rationale: The cited paper is used in the theory-building section of Study 3B, where the authors explicitly rely on prior evidence that consumers distort option attributes under difficult trade-offs, especially when making a purchase rather than merely browsing. They state they are "building on this research" to motivate their browse-versus-purchase prediction and mechanism test. This is more than a passing literature list mention because it informs the conceptual setup of a key experiment.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the cited paper's predecisional distortion insight to a gift-giving context. It uses that logic to predict and test when close givers will perceptually distort registry versus nonregistry options.
